description: Whether the provider ships default/built-in commands alongside user-defined custom commands.

claude-code.mechanism: extensive set of built-in slash commands (/help, /clear, /compact, /config, /model, /mcp, /memory, /plan, etc.) plus bundled-skill commands (/batch, /debug, /loop); not user-modifiable

cline.mechanism: Built-in slash commands (e.g., /newtask, /smol alias /compact, /newrule, /deep-planning, /explain-changes in VS Code, /reportbug) are hardcoded and not user-modifiable; user-authored workflows are additive, invoked alongside built-ins

codex.mechanism: no implementation details captured; slash commands feature exists but format and capabilities are undocumented in cached sources

copilot-cli.mechanism: Copilot CLI has no standalone slash-command mechanism; extensibility is via plugins that bundle agents/skills/hooks; command format within plugins is undocumented

factory-droid.mechanism: no built-in slash commands documented; Factory commands are user-defined Markdown templates or executables

gemini-cli.mechanism: built-in slash commands include /commands reload (reload command files without restart) and /help; user-defined commands supplement but do not replace built-ins

opencode.mechanism: OpenCode documentation describes only user-authored slash commands loaded from .opencode/commands/ and ~/.config/opencode/commands/; no built-in commands are documented for this namespace.

pi.mechanism: pi prompt templates are entirely user-defined; no built-in slash commands documented

roo-code.mechanism: Roo Code commands are user-defined Markdown files in .roo/commands/ — no user-overridable built-in slash-command layer documented

windsurf.mechanism: Cascade exposes its own built-in chat slash commands (e.g. for conversation control) independently of user-authored Workflows; the `/<workflow-name>` namespace is reserved for user files
